methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's partnership with ed2go to provide on-demand workforce training suggests distributed capability-building infrastructure reaching beyond metro hubs, though depth depends on whether training cultivates genuine AI fluency or platform-level familiarity.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure specifically targeting Richmond's young workforce signals deepening capability concentration in metro areas where employers demand AI skills, potentially widening the gap with regions where such pressure doesn't exist.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA explicitly aims to distribute AI education and community impact beyond elite institutions, representing intentional infrastructure for broad, deep fluency rather than platform dependency.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: AI integration in Hampton Roads real estate through turnkey tools signals shallow, platform-mediated adoption in traditional industries—broad distribution without deep technical capability or custom development.

signals.title: Q&A: Who’s responsible when AI makes mistakes? - UVA Today
signals.signal_reading: UVA's engagement with AI accountability questions positions it as a deep knowledge hub for AI governance and ethics, but concentration of such expertise in elite universities risks leaving most Virginia institutions and communities without frameworks for responsible AI deployment.
